content:
So recently I made a client side tool (runs entirely in the browser) that lets you create movie bar codes. it runs client side with ffmpeg.wasm, nothing is uploaded and everything is processed on the client device. Threw a 2.5GB MKV at it and it worked fine.
But it's slow. A 45min episode takes 10-15 min. A full movie can be over an hour.
What suggestions do you have that can allow me to speed it up?
I have tried MT ffmpeg and it doesn't seems to work or doesn't use all the threads in my testing.
I have tried to only process only keyframes but still too slow.
AI suggested using WebCodecs, is that the only solution to this problem? Is ffmpeg.wsam not the right tool?
Would love your suggestion. Quite an engineering problem as I am stuck with the limitations of WSAM.
Test Here

  submitted by   /u/hex_code_seven
[link]   [comments]
